---
title: "exp1"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Load the Data

```{r}
library(GenSA)
library(tidyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
data <- read_excel("/Users/foxxis/Documents/social induction/experiment data/exp1/output100.xlsx")
data


#add a new id to everyone
data <- data %>% 
  mutate(id=paste0('p', row_number()))
data

```

## Descriptive Analysis

```{r}
final_data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    age = as.numeric(age),
    education = as.numeric(education),
    gender = as.factor(gender)
  )


#turn to numeric 
final_data <- final_data %>%
  mutate(
    div = as.numeric(av_pdiv)*2,
    nodiv = as.numeric(av_nodiv)*2
  )

rate_data <- final_data %>%
  select( div, nodiv)

# descriptive analysis
summary_stats <- summary(rate_data)
print(summary_stats)

sd_value <- sd(rate_data$div, na.rm = TRUE)
cat("SD div:", sd_value, "\n")

sd_value <- sd(rate_data$nodiv, na.rm = TRUE)
cat("SD nodiv:", sd_value, "\n")

div_bigger <- sum(final_data$div > final_data$nodiv, na.rm = TRUE)
nodiv_bigger <- sum(final_data$div < final_data$nodiv, na.rm = TRUE)
equal <- sum(final_data$div == final_data$nodiv, na.rm = TRUE)
cat("Div > NoDiv:", div_bigger/101,  "\n")
cat("Nodiv > Div:", nodiv_bigger/101, "\n")
cat("Div = NoDiv:", equal/101, "\n")

```

```{r}

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(rstatix)
library(ggpubr)


plot_data <- final_data %>%
  select(ip, div, nodiv) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(div, nodiv),
               names_to = "type",
               values_to = "value")


# Normality (per condition)
plot_data  %>% group_by(type) %>% shapiro_test(value)

# Repeated-measures ANOVA
# Mauchly’s test for sphericity & GG/HF corrections
anova_res <- anova_test(
  data = plot_data, dv = value, wid = ip, within = type,
  effect.size = "pes"
)
get_anova_table(anova_res, correction = "GG")


# Effect sizes (paired Cohen's d)
es <- plot_data %>%
  cohens_d(value ~ type, paired = TRUE, var.equal = FALSE)

es


# Plot: each participants as each line
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = type, y = value, group = ip)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.3) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("div", "nodiv")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Each Participant's Line Across div, nodiv",
       x = "Rate Type", y = "Value")
```

## Significance Test

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggpubr)
library(rstatix)
#ANOVA
t1non <- final_data$d2
t1div <- final_data$d1
t2non <- final_data$d4
t2div <- final_data$d3
t3non <- final_data$d8
t3div <- final_data$d7
t4non <- final_data$d6
t4div <- final_data$d5
#calculate the difference within pairs
t1dif <- t1div -t1non
t2dif <- -(t2div-t2non)
t3dif <- -t3div+t3non #Notably, we standardize all choice data by turn trials favoring Jar II into Jar I
t4dif <- -t4div+t4non

data <- data.frame(
  participant = factor(1:length(t1dif)),
  t1 = t1dif,  # vote=yes, model=yes
  t2 = t2dif,  # vote=no,  model=yes
  t3 = t3dif,  # vote=yes,  model=no
  t4 = t4dif   # vote=no, model=no
)

library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)

# Create long data for 2x2 repeated-measure anova
long_data <- data %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("t"), names_to = "condition", values_to = "diff") %>%
  mutate(
    vote  = ifelse(condition %in% c("t1", "t3"), "yes", "no"),
    model = ifelse(condition %in% c("t1", "t2"), "yes", "no"),
    vote  = factor(vote),
    model = factor(model)
  )


#descriptive analysis
long_data  %>%
  group_by(vote, model) %>%
  get_summary_stats(diff, type = "mean_sd")

bxp <- ggboxplot(
  long_data, x = "vote", y = "diff",
  color = "model", palette = "jco"
  )
bxp

#shapiro test
long_data %>%
  group_by(vote,model) %>%
  shapiro_test(diff)

ggqqplot(long_data, "diff", ggtheme = theme_bw()) +
  facet_grid(vote ~ model, labeller = "label_both") #Although the normality assumption is violated, the deviation is not severe. Given the large sample size (n = 101 ≫ 30), we proceeded with the analysis under the assumption of normality.


#ANOVA Result
aov_results <- long_data %>%
  anova_test(
    dv = diff,
    wid = participant,
    within = c(vote, model)
  )
get_anova_table(aov_results)

pwc_model_within_vote <- long_data %>%
  group_by(vote) %>%
  pairwise_t_test(
    diff ~ model,
    paired = TRUE,
    p.adjust.method = "bonferroni"
  )

pwc_vote_within_model <- long_data %>%
  group_by(model) %>%
  pairwise_t_test(
    diff ~ vote,
    paired = TRUE,
    p.adjust.method = "bonferroni"
  )

pwc_model_within_vote
pwc_vote_within_model

# Visualization: box plots with p-values
pwc_model_within_vote <- pwc_model_within_vote %>% 
  add_xy_position(x = "vote")

# Create your base plot
bxp + 
  labs(
    caption = get_pwc_label(pwc_model_within_vote)
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0.5),  
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black", size = 1),  
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),  
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),  
    axis.line = element_line(size = 0.5, color = "black")
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10, face = "italic")  
  )
#Finally, one sample t-test for each condition
print(t.test(t1dif, mu=0))
print(t.test(t2dif, mu=0))
print(t.test(t3dif, mu=0))
print(t.test(t4dif, mu=0))

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

long_data
p<-ggplot(long_data, aes(x=vote, y=diff, fill=model))+
  geom_boxplot()
 

p+scale_fill_manual(values=c("gold", "#48D1CC"))+
  theme_classic()+
  geom_segment(aes(x = 1, xend = 2, y = 3.5, yend = 3.5), color = "black", linewidth = 1) +  
  geom_text(aes(x = 1.5, y = 3.7, label = "***"), size = 6, color = "black") +
  theme(
    panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1)  
  )

```
